item.title: The Enormous Financial Cost of Three Weeks of War in Iran
item.description: An estimated $11 billion was spent in just the first six days.

item.title: Asia Refiners Ask Saudis to Change Oil Pricing System Due to War
item.description: Asian oil refiners have asked Saudi Arabia to change the way it prices the crude they take after the Middle East war disrupted the traditional system, according to traders.

item.title: Watch Exports Bounced Back in February Before War in Middle East
item.description: Swiss watch exports returned to growth last month as US demand continued an uneven recovery from the fallout over trade tariffs.

item.title: CK Hutchison Profit Misses, With Outlook Dented By Iran War
item.description: CK Hutchison Holdings Ltd. reported a weaker-than-expected profit for 2025, even as the Iran conflict ripples through global trade and adds fresh strain to the conglomerate’s ports and retail businesses.

item.title: SNB Restates Increased Resolve on Franc as Rate Held at Zero
item.description: The Swiss National Bank restated its heightened readiness to sell the franc as officials held back again from the more drastic step of cutting borrowing costs into negative territory.

item.title: Sweden Holds Interest Rates Steady as Iran War Clouds Outlook
item.description: Sweden’s central bank held borrowing costs unchanged for a fourth straight meeting and restated a plan for no changes this year as it awaits more clarity on impacts from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Shell Says Pearl GTL Plant Sustained Damage in Attack Wednesday
item.description: Shell Plc’s Pearl gas-to-liquids plant sustained damage in Iran’s attack on the Ras Laffan Industrial City on Wednesday, the oil major said.

item.title: Trump Seeks End to Attacks on Energy Sites as Gas Fields Burn
item.description: US President Donald Trump sought to stop attacks on energy facilities in the Middle East after Israel and Iran struck key targets, fueling concern that a prolonged war will cause lasting damage to oil and gas infrastructure.

item.title: Hearing-Aid Stocks Face Uncertain Road After Game-Changing Deal
item.description: A potentially game-changing deal in the hearing-aid industry is poised to increase pressure on some of Europe’s worst-performing health care shares.

item.title: Samsung to Spend $73 Billion on Chip Expansion, Research in 2026
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. plans to spend more than 110 trillion won ($73.3 billion) on capital expenditure and research in 2026, reflecting expansions in memory chip capacity as well as exploration into fields such as AI.

item.title: How War in Iran Will Squeeze America's Farmers Even Further
item.description: More challenges are on the way.

item.title: LSEG Looks to Deepen European Foothold in Equity Trading Unit
item.description: London Stock Exchange Group Plc is boosting its European presence as it named senior executives to lead its Turquoise trading venue.

item.title: KNDS in Talks With Middle East Customers on Drone Defense
item.description: Franco-German defense firm KNDS NV is in talks with clients in the Middle East to provide additional equipment for drone defense, Chief Executive Officer Jean-Paul Alary said.

item.title: Odd Lots: War in Iran Squeezes America’s Farmers Again (Podcast)
item.description: America’s farmers can’t seem to catch a break. Years of thin margins and rising costs have already stretched them to the limit. And now, war with Iran is making things even harder. The conflict is driving up global energy and fertilizer prices, pushing producers into tough decisions about what to plant and at what price to sell. At the same time, farmers are still dealing with the impact of tariffs, rising land costs, and stiff competition from agricultural powerhouses like Brazil. On this episo

item.title: Foreign Investors Flee Japanese Stocks as Oil Risk Dims Outlook
item.description: Foreigners turned net sellers of Japanese stocks for the first time in 2026 last week amid growing worries that higher oil prices will hit the nation’s economy.

item.title: Asia Buys Most US Oil in Three Years as War Blocks Mideast Flows
item.description: Asian buyers have scooped up the most US oil in three years this month as they hunt for alternatives to Persian Gulf crude trapped behind the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK Jobs Market Fares Better Than Feared Ahead of BOE Meeting
item.description: UK unemployment held steady and employers hired more staff in early signs that the labor market may be stabilizing ahead of the Bank of England’s interest-rate decision later today.

item.title: European Gas Jumps 35% After World’s Top LNG Plant Hit By Iran
item.description: European natural gas prices jumped after Iran intensified attacks to energy infrastructure in the Gulf, causing damage to the world’s largest liquefied natural gas export plant.

item.title: Berlin DJ Tech Firm Woos Buyers After Private Credit Walks Away
item.description: The administrator of Native Instruments Group is nearing a sale of the music technology firm after its private credit lenders shied away from a takeover that could have averted an insolvency filing.

item.title: FTSE 100 Futures Fall, Pound Gains
North Korea FX Revenue Boosted by Russia Arms Sales, Cyber Crime
item.description: North Korea’s foreign currency earnings have reached their highest levels since before sweeping sanctions were imposed in 2018 over its banned weapons programs, driven by cyber-heists and arms sales to Russia, US intelligence authorities said.

item.title: Matsui Securities in Talks With Finance Firms on Capital Tie-Up
item.description: Matsui Securities Ltd., one of Japan’s pioneers for online trading, has begun talks with several major domestic financial institutions over a possible capital alliance, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Paras Healthcare Is Said to Revive Up to $200 Million India IPO
item.description: Paras Healthcare Ltd. has revived plans for an initial public offering and hired three banks for a share sale that could raise as much as $200 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Top European Stock Fund Finds Bargains During Iran War Selloff
item.description: The manager of a top-performing European equity fund said the pullback in markets since the start of the war in Iran has allowed her to pick up under-the-radar stocks at deeper discounts.

item.title: Czech Rates to Stay Put as Policymakers Lean on Inflation Buffer
item.description: Czech policymakers are poised to keep interest rates on hold as inflation running below target provides a cushion against the immediate impact of surging oil costs.

item.title: India’s Net Forward Dollar Sales to Aid Rupee Near $100 Billion
item.description: India ramped up use of a key tool for defending the rupee to record levels as the currency weakened to an all-time low against the dollar, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nomura Wholesale Chief Says War Adds to Case for April BOJ Hike
item.description: Nomura Holdings Inc.’s wholesale business head expects the Bank of Japan to raise interest rates next month, even as the effects of the Iran war cloud the outlook for the economy.

item.title: Emerging Assets Fall for First Time This Week as Oil Prices Jump
item.description: Emerging market equities and currencies fell for the first time this week as oil prices surged after attacks on some of the Middle East’s most important energy facilities.

item.title: Powell Digs In as War Forces a New Path for the Fed
item.description: The embattled chairman raises the stakes against prosecution.

item.title: Bank of Japan Decision Puts Yen-160 Watch in Focus, Analysts Say
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s widely expected decision to hold its benchmark rate amid risks from the Iran war left the yen little changed, though analysts said Governor Kazuo Ueda’s remarks later would be key to whether the currency would swing past 160.
